qa_pairs.question: What is the command to run a COMSOL batch job?
qa_pairs.answer: You can run a COMSOL batch job using the command `comsol batch -inputfile ${INPUTFILE} -outputfile ${OUTPUTFILE} -np $SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE`.

qa_pairs.question: Does COMSOL always efficiently use a large number of cores?
qa_pairs.answer: No, depending on the complexity of the simulation, COMSOL may not be able to efficiently use very many cores.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended to determine the optimal number of cores for a COMSOL simulation?
qa_pairs.answer: It is advisable to test the scaling of your simulation by gradually increasing the number of cores.

qa_pairs.question: When should a COMSOL job be considered for execution across multiple compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: If near-linear speedup is obtained when using all cores on a single compute node, you should consider running the job over multiple full nodes.
